channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 2,000 U.S. Troops From 82nd Airborne Division to Be Sent to Middle East
item.description: The order gives President Trump more military options as he considers diplomacy with Iran. It is unclear where the soldiers will go in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Getty Images
item.media:description: Members of the 82nd Airborne Division in North Carolina in 2021. It is unclear where roughly 2,000 soldiers from the division will go in the Middle East, but the location would be within striking distance of Iran, officials said.

item.title: United States Said to Have Sent Iran a Plan to End the Middle East War
item.description: The 15-point plan was delivered via Pakistan, whose army chief has emerged as the key interlocutor between the United States and Iran, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rasgon, Julian E. Barnes and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
item.media:description: An apartment shattered by an airstrike in Tehran on Monday.

item.title: Iran Says ‘Non-Hostile’ Ships Can Sail Through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Ships with no ties to Israel or the United States would be allowed to pass, the government said, but it was unclear if any vessels would try.
item.dc:creator: Peter Eavis
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Reuters
item.media:description: Cargo ships in the Gulf, near the Strait of Hormuz, seen from the United Arab Emirates earlier this month.

item.title: Senate Republicans Again Block Bid to Halt Iran War Without Authorization
item.description: Senate Democrats failed for the third time to advance a resolution that would force the removal of troops from hostilities in Iran unless Congress approves offensive operations.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senator Christopher S. Murphy, Democrat of Connecticut, introduced Tuesday’s war powers resolution.

item.title: Air Canada Pilots Killed in LaGuardia Crash Were Early in Flying Careers
item.description: The pilots were identified as Antoine Forest, 30, and Mackenzie Gunther. They were the only two fatalities in the plane’s collision with a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia Airport.
item.dc:creator: Pranav Baskar, Vjosa Isai, Hurubie Meko and Norimitsu Onishi
item.media:credit: Dakota Santiago for The New York Times
item.media:description: The wreckage of an Air Canada Express jet after it collided with a fire truck at LaGuardia Airport on Sunday.

item.title: LaGuardia Crash Tests New Port Authority Leader
item.description: Kathryn Garcia, who began leading the agency in February, consoled two injured firefighters and dealt with delays at the three big airports serving New York City.
item.dc:creator: Patrick McGeehan
item.category: Port Authority of New York and New Jersey
item.media:credit: Victor J. Blue for The New York Times
item.media:description: Kathryn Garcia, the executive director of the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, met with two employees who were injured in the collision.

item.title: Democrat Emily Gregory Wins Florida Special Election in Mar-a-Lago’s District
item.description: Emily Gregory’s victory in Palm Beach brought the Democratic surge to President Trump’s backyard, while a union leader leads in a race for a state senate seat vacated by Florida’s lieutenant governor.
item.dc:creator: David W. Chen
item.media:credit: Josh Ritchie for The New York Times
item.media:description: Emily Gregory’s district includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate in Palm Beach, Fla.

item.title: N.C. Senator Phil Berger Officially Just Lost to Sam Page
item.description: Phil Berger had money, power and an endorsement from President Trump. But his critics had a long list of resentments and, on Tuesday, he lost by a mere 23 votes.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina
item.media:credit: Cornell Watson for The New York Times
item.media:description: Phil Berger is the country’s longest-serving active State Senate leader.

item.title: Newsom Says He Regrets Remarks Comparing Israel to ‘Apartheid State’
item.description: The California governor suggested that he had meant to refer to Israel’s potential future direction, not its current policies. “I revere the state of Israel,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina
item.media:credit: Godofredo A. Vásquez/Associated Press
item.media:description: Gov. Gavin Newsom of California has long been a supporter of Israel, but he has stepped up his criticism of its government more recently.

item.title: Trump and Republican National Committee Lean Toward Dallas for Unusual 2026 GOP Midterm Convention
item.description: Parties normally hold conventions every four years to nominate presidential candidates, but Republicans hope to hold one this year in the face of midterm headwinds.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Julio Cortez/Associated Press
item.media:description: President Trump has pushed for a major gathering before this year’s midterm elections to motivate his supporters, and Republicans are close to settling on Dallas as the location.

item.title: Republicans Step Up Efforts to Reach DHS Funding Deal and End Shutdown
item.description: A day after President Trump said he did not want a compromise, Senate Republicans sent Democrats a plan that would break off ICE enforcement funding and reopen the rest of the department.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Senate Republicans said they would seek to fund ICE enforcement operations through a separate bill that would not be subject to a Democratic filibuster.

item.title: TSA Airport Delays: What It’s Like Waiting in Line for Hours
item.description: Scenes from extra long lines at two airports, and a surprising discovery at a third.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder, Shannon Sims and Mark Bonamo
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
item.media:description: Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport employees directing passengers in line at a Transportation Security Administration checkpoint on Monday.

item.title: Wicked Stepmother No Longer, a Female Pharoah Gets a Reputational Makeover
item.description: A reassessment of damaged 3,500-year-old statuary adds to evidence that Queen Hatshepsut wasn’t the villain that scholars long took her to be.
item.dc:creator: Franz Lidz

item.title: Trump Picks ‘Alpha Male’ Influencer to Be Tourism Envoy
item.description: Nick Adams, known for his crass humor and internet trolling, was previously nominated to be the ambassador to Malaysia. But his nomination was pulled earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Andy Dossett/Examiner-Enterprise, via Imagn Images
item.media:description: President Trump picked Nick Adams for a newly created position: the “special presidential envoy for American tourism, exceptionalism and values.”

item.title: Key Adviser Quits Federal Vaccine Panel
item.description: Dr. Robert Malone, vice chair of the committee, was appointed by Robert F. Kennedy Jr. after a purge of the previous advisers.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
item.media:description: Dr. Robert Malone, the vice chair of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices, during a meeting at the C.D.C. in Atlanta in December.

item.title: Delta Air Lines Says It Will Suspend Special Services for Congress Members
item.description: Airport escorts and “red coat” assistance for lawmakers will be suspended, the airline said on Tuesday, citing the extended partial government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka
item.media:credit: Stefani Reynolds/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Delta Air Lines said that it would suspend special services for members of Congress, amid a stalemate over funding for the Department of Homeland Security.

item.title: Supreme Court Seems Open to Trump Request to Block Asylum Seekers at Border
item.description: A policy of turning back many asylum seekers at the border was rescinded in 2021, but the Justice Department wants the flexibility to reinstate it as a tool for border control.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:credit: Alejandro Cegarra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Migrants approved for asylum appointments with Customs and Border Protection lined up for permission to travel to the U.S. in Tapachula, Mexico, last year.

item.title: T.S.A. Tipped Off ICE Agents Before Arrests at San Francisco Airport
item.description: Transportation Security Administration officials told ICE that a mother and daughter under a detention order had planned to fly domestically, federal documents show.
item.dc:creator: Hamed Aleaziz and Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Jeff Chiu/Associated Press
item.media:description: Angelina Lopez-Jimenez and her 9-year-old daughter were detained at the San Francisco International Airport and later deported to Guatemala.

item.title: Somali Immigrants Have Effectively Been Denied a Fair Hearing, Lawsuit Says
item.description: Two Minnesota-based legal groups accused the Justice Department of unconstitutionally expediting Somalis’ immigration cases.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
item.media:description: The vast majority of the 80,000 Somalis who live in Minnesota are American citizens or legal permanent residents.

item.title: The U.S. Military Said It Helped Bomb a Drug Camp in Ecuador. It Was a Dairy Farm.
item.description: The Times visited a village where the United States and Ecuador said they destroyed an armed group’s training camp. Residents said it was actually a dairy farm.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, José María León Cabrera, Annie Correal, Eric Schmitt and Federico Rios
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: The remains of a dairy cattle farm in San Martín that was burned and bombed by the Ecuadorean military.

item.title: San Francisco Killed 8th-Grade Algebra. Now It’s Set to Come Back.
item.description: The San Francisco school board approved a plan to restore algebra as an option at all middle schools, more than a decade after it was removed over equity concerns.
item.dc:creator: Dana Goldstein
item.media:credit: Liz Hafalia/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
item.media:description: The decision to stop offering algebra in middle school frustrated some families in the San Francisco Unified School District.

item.title: Russia Launches Daytime Attack on Ukraine With Over 500 Drones
item.description: The assault, which came after overnight strikes across the country, was one of the largest of the war, the Ukrainian authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Cassandra Vinograd
item.media:credit: Mykola Tys/Associated Press

item.title: Russian Oil Shipment Puts Focus on Kremlin Spy Outpost in Cuba
item.description: Moscow may be challenging President Trump’s effort to choke Cuba’s economy. China also has suspected listening posts on the island.
item.dc:creator: Michael Crowley
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Norlys Perez/Reuters
item.media:description: Mr. Trump has spoken of using his economic leverage to mount a “friendly takeover” of Cuba and is pressing for the ouster of its president, Miguel Díaz-Canel.

item.title: Meta Ordered to Pay $375 Million Over Child Safety Violations
item.description: In one of the company’s first major losses, New Mexico jurors found that it had misled consumers about the safety of its platforms, enabling sexual exploitation of young users.
item.dc:creator: Cecilia Kang and Eli Tan
item.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Meta, which is led by Mark Zuckerberg, said it would appeal a verdict in state court in Santa Fe, N.M.

item.title: OpenAI Is Shutting Down Sora, Its A.I. Video Generator
item.description: The start-up said it would discontinue Sora just three months after signing a multiyear deal to bring Disney characters to the service.
item.dc:creator: Cade Metz

item.title: Hawaii Storms Bring More Rain to Oahu and Maui
item.description: Experts agree that the past weeks have been one of the rainiest periods in recent memory across the state.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff
item.media:credit: Eli Pace/The Maui News, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A storm last weekend damaged a road in Maui County, Hawaii.

item.title: The War Is Going Better Than You Think
item.description: How the fight in Iran compares to past conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Naila Ruechel for The New York Times

item.title: For Putin, the War in Iran Changed Everything
item.description: In one swoop, it overturned the conditions for conciliation.
item.dc:creator: Mikhail Zygar
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Alexander Zemlianichenko

item.title: Late Night Questions the ‘Very Big Present’ Trump Got From Iran
item.description: Josh Johnson was puzzled by what kind of gift the president could have received “from the people you are currently at war with.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Plan to Disarm Hamas in Gaza Is Detailed at U.N.
item.description: A Board of Peace member said the most dangerous weapons would be collected first. He linked compliance with disarmament to reconstruction beginning in the enclave.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: Hamas fighters in Gaza City in November.

item.title: Massachusetts Teacher Charged With Raping Two Students
item.description: The students say the former teacher at Miss Hall’s, a boarding school, groomed them and began having sex with them at age 16, which state law considers old enough for consent.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Russell
item.media:credit: David Degner for The New York Times
item.media:description: Matthew Rutledge, who was indicted on three counts of rape on Tuesday, taught at Miss Hall’s school in Pittsfield, Mass.

item.title: Sarah Mullally, the First Female Archbishop of Canterbury, is About to Be Enthroned
item.description: Sarah Mullally will be installed on Wednesday at Canterbury Cathedral. Her appointment to the role has been both celebrated, and denounced by some factions within the global Anglican Church.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Jeff Moore
item.media:description: The ceremony in Canterbury on Wednesday is the symbolic start of Sarah Mullally’s ministry, after she was legally confirmed as archbishop at St. Paul’s Cathedral in London in January.
